evidence_summary: Graph neural networks can utilize graph structure to improve node classification performance by encoding the graph structure directly using a neural network model and training on a supervised target for all nodes with labels.
A simple and well-behaved layer-wise propagation rule for neural network models can be used to operate directly on graphs and learn representations of nodes both with and without labels.
Graph-based neural network models can be used for fast and scalable semi-supervised classification of nodes in a graph, and can outperform related methods by a significant margin.

Unsupervised learning approaches, such as Deep Graph Infomax (DGI), can be used to learn node representations within graph-structured data and achieve competitive performance on node classification benchmarks.
